summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the Higgs boson?

The Higgs Boson The Higgs field gives mass to fundamental particles. The other options — 'The heaviest quark', 'A type of lepton', and 'The carrier of gravity' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is antimatter?

Antimatter — Every particle has an antiparticle (same mass, opposite charge). The Higgs Boson The Higgs field gives mass to fundamental particles. The other options — 'Particles with no mass', 'Dark matter', and 'Particles moving backward in time' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: What happens when a particle meets its antiparticle?

When they meet — annihilation → pure energy. The other options — 'They pass through each other', 'They form a nucleus', and 'Nothing happens' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
